---

# press Usage

`press` is a single-binary static site generator. It turns structured Markdown files into a clean static website — no config files, no plugin ecosystem.

## When to Use

- Setting up a new press project from scratch
- Creating, updating, or deleting pages and sections
- Building or locally serving a press site
- Understanding the project layout or frontmatter fields
- Choosing or switching built-in themes
- Using Markdown extensions (YouTube embeds, tables, footnotes, etc.)
- Scripting or automating press as part of a pipeline

## Installation

### One-liner (Linux & macOS)

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ChristianKreuzberger/press/main/install.sh | bash
```

Detects OS and CPU architecture automatically, installs to `/usr/local/bin` (or `~/.local/bin` without root).

### Build from source

```bash
go install github.com/ChristianKreuzberger/press@latest
```

### Verify

```bash
press --version
```

---

## Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Scaffold a new site
press init

# 2. Create pages
press create page about
press create page blog/my-first-post   # nested inside a section

# 3. Edit content
# Edit pages/about.md with your editor

# 4. Build
press build

# 5. Open dist/index.html or deploy the dist/ folder
```

---

## All Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `press init [dir] [--theme name]` | Scaffold a new site (`template.html` + `pages/`); choose a built-in theme |
| `press list page` | List all pages |
| `press create page <name> [--file f.md]` | Create a page; `name` may include sections (e.g. `blog/my-post`, `blog/2026/my-post`) |
| `press update page <name> --file f.md` | Replace a page's content |
| `press delete page <name>` | Delete a page |
| `press list section` | List all sections |
| `press create section <name> [--file f.md]` | Create a section (folder + `index.md`) |
| `press update section <name> --file f.md` | Replace a section's index content |
| `press delete section <name>` | Delete a section and all its pages |
| `press build [-output dir]` | Build the site into `dist/` (default) |
| `press serve [-port N] [-output dir]` | Build and serve locally; rebuilds on file changes |
| `press tree` | Show a tree of all pages and sections |
| `press --version` | Print the installed version |

Run any command with `--help` for detailed usage.

---

## Project Layout

```
my-site/
├── template.html        # HTML theme — {{.Title}} and {{.Content}} are injected
├── pages/
│   ├── index.md         # → dist/index.html
│   ├── about.md         # → dist/about.html
│   └── blog/            # Section (subdirectory)
│       ├── index.md     # → dist/blog/index.html  (section landing page)
│       └── my-post.md   # → dist/blog/my-post.html
└── dist/                # Generated output (created by `press build`)
```

- **Pages** live directly under `pages/` → top-level HTML files.
- **Sections** are subdirectories under `pages/`. Each must contain an `index.md`.
- Deploy the `dist/` folder to any static host (Netlify, GitHub Pages, S3, etc.).

---

## Frontmatter

Every `.md` file starts with YAML frontmatter (auto-generated by `press create`):

```yaml
---
title: "My Page"
alias: ""
tags: []
weight: 0
created_at: "2026-04-22T10:00:00Z"
updated_at: "2026-04-22T10:00:00Z"
---
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `title` | string | Display name used in navigation and `<title>` |
| `alias` | string | Alternative URL slug (reserved) |
| `tags` | list | Content tags (reserved) |
| `weight` | integer | Navigation order — lower = first; `0` = sorted after weighted items |
| `created_at` | RFC 3339 | Creation timestamp |
| `updated_at` | RFC 3339 | Last-updated timestamp |

### Ordering with `weight`

```yaml
---
title: "About"
weight: 1   # appears first in nav
---
```

Pages with `weight: 0` (default) appear after all weighted items in filesystem order.

### Section frontmatter (`index.md` only)

`press create section <name>` scaffolds an `index.md` with two additional fields:

```yaml
---
title: "Blog"
alias: ""
tags: []
weight: 0
created_at: "2026-04-22T10:00:00Z"
updated_at: "2026-04-22T10:00:00Z"
toc_sort: "weight"
toc_order: "asc"
---
```

| Field | Type | Values | Description |
|-------|------|--------|-------------|
| `toc_sort` | string | `weight`, `title`, `created_at`, `updated_at` | Field to sort child pages/sub-sections by in the TOC |
| `toc_order` | string | `asc`, `desc` | Sort direction |

`weight: 0` always sorts last, regardless of `toc_order`.

---

## Template Variables

The `template.html` file receives the following variables:

| Variable | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `{{.Title}}` | string | Page title from frontmatter |
| `{{.Content}}` | HTML | Rendered HTML content of the page |
| `{{.Pages}}` | `[]PageRef` | Global navigation: all top-level pages + section indexes, sorted by weight |
| `{{.TableOfContents}}` | `[]TOCEntry` | Section child pages/sub-sections for the current section's `index.md`; empty for all other pages |

### `PageRef` fields

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `.Title` | Page or section title |
| `.URL` | Relative URL (e.g. `about.html`, `blog/index.html`) |

### `TOCEntry` fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `.Title` | string | Page or sub-section title |
| `.URL` | string | URL relative to the section index (e.g. `my-post.html`, `sub/index.html`) |
| `.CreatedAt` | `time.Time` | Creation timestamp from frontmatter |
| `.UpdatedAt` | `time.Time` | Last-updated timestamp from frontmatter |

### Table of contents example

```html
{{if .TableOfContents}}
<section class="toc">
  <h2>Contents</h2>
  <ul>
    {{range .TableOfContents}}
    <li><a href="{{.URL}}">{{.Title}}</a></li>
    {{end}}
  </ul>
</section>
{{end}}
```

The default template already renders this block automatically after `.Content`.

---

## Themes

press ships three built-in themes. Choose one when scaffolding a new site:

```bash
press init --theme dark      # default — GitHub-inspired dark mode
press init --theme light     # clean editorial style with serif headings
press init --theme terminal  # retro green-on-black, all monospace
```

If `--theme` is omitted, `dark` is used. You can also fall back later:

```bash
press init /tmp/theme-preview --theme light
cp /tmp/theme-preview/template.html ./template.html
```

`template.html` is a standard Go `html/template` file — edit it freely or replace it with your own design.

---

## Markdown Extensions

press supports standard GitHub-Flavored Markdown (GFM) plus a few extras.

### GFM features included

- **Tables** — pipe-delimited tables
- **Task lists** — `- [ ] item` / `- [x] item`
- **Strikethrough** — `~~text~~`
- **Definition lists**
- **Footnotes** — `[^1]` references

### YouTube embed

Place `!youtube[VIDEO_ID]` on its own line to embed a responsive player:

```markdown
!youtube[dQw4w9WgXcQ]
```

The `VIDEO_ID` is the 11-character identifier from the YouTube URL. Renders as a privacy-enhanced `youtube-nocookie.com` iframe at 16:9. The shortcode is left as-is inside fenced code blocks or inline code spans.

---

## Key Behaviours

- Output goes to `dist/` by default; override with `-output <dir>`.
- `press serve` watches for file changes and rebuilds automatically.
- Errors go to `stderr`; content goes to `stdout` or disk — easy to script.
- No telemetry, no internet connection required after installation.
